title: Introduction to AFT
body_text:
Introduction to AFT
1	AFT Overview
1.1	Overview to Originating Automated Funds Transfer (AFT)
Automated Funds Transfers (AFT) are electronic credit and debit transactions that are authorized by an account holder to credit or debit their account, usually at regular intervals. Typical AFT transactions include payroll deposits (credits), strata corporation fee payments (debits), and loan or mortgage payments (debits). The Originator (financial institution or business member) can create Canadian or US dollar transactions if the recipientâs account is held at a Canadian financial institution.
Central 1 offers the following options to facilitate the processing of AFT transactions:
PaymentStreamâ¢
AFT application on
PaymentStream
Direct
File transmission
The
PaymentStream
AFT systems allow Originators to generate AFT transactions and to release AFT files either automatically or manually. For more information, see the Originating AFT via
PaymentStream
AFT Part.
Alternatively, Originators can use in-house or third-party software to generate AFT files. The files are uploaded to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange or
PaymentStream
AFT. See the Originating AFT via File Transmission (BC) Part or the Originating AFT via
PaymentStream
AFT Part, as applicable, for more information.
Once Central 1 receives an AFT file, itâs validated and forwarded to the data centre of the receiving financial institution. The transaction is either credited or debited to the appropriate customerâs account on the due date.
Invalid AFT transactions can be rejected at Central 1 or at the destination financial institution. Other exceptions include AFT recalls on unposted AFT transactions, and error corrections on posted AFT transactions. See the AFT Rejects, Recalls, Error Corrections, and Other Exception Items Part for more information.
To dishonour incoming AFT transactions, use the Online Return System (ORS) or alternate software. See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for more information.
Central 1 generates various reports that financial institutions and business members use to review AFT transactions, including rejected and returned items. US dollar transactions are reported separately. See the AFT Reports Part for more information.
Related Information
AFT Rejects, Recalls, Error Corrections, and Other Exception Items Part
AFT Returns and AFT Notice of Change Transactions Part
AFT Settlement Part
AFT Reports Part
File Exchange and Report Distribution Part
Originating AFT via File Transmission (BC) Part
Originating AFT via PaymentStream AFT Part
Service Charges Part
User Management Part
1.2	Summary of Originating AFT Processes
This section describes how AFT files and AFT transactions are processed.
Creating AFT Files
The Originator, either a financial institution or business member, creates AFT transactions directly on
PaymentStream
AFT or on alternate software. If the transactions are input on
PaymentStream
AFT, the file is released manually or automatically.
If AFT transactions are created on alternate software, the AFT file is uploaded to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange or to
PaymentStream
AFT. The Originator must verify that the file is successfully submitted.
Processing AFT Files at Central 1
Once an AFT file is received at Central 1, it is processed as follows:
File Verification
â
Central 1 verifies the file format, File Creation Number, File Limit, and Originator ID. If the entire file is rejected, Central 1 advises the Originator to correct and re-transmit the file. However, it is the Originatorâs responsibility to ensure that the AFT file is successfully received at Central 1.
A confirmation of receipt appears in the .LST file if the AFT file is uploaded to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange. Alternatively, the Originator receives a confirmation email if the AFT file is uploaded to
PaymentStream
AFT.
Transaction Verification
â
Central 1âs AFT system identifies invalid transactions. If an individual transaction is invalid, it is deleted from the file. The entire file is not rejected. AFT transactions that are rejected appear on the Consolidated Returns, Recalls, Rejects, and Error Corrections Report â ICRR/UCRR.
Central 1 forwards a file of accepted AFT transactions to the data centre of each receiving financial institution. In some cases, Central 1 also delivers the offsetting entry to the financial institutionâs banking system. In some circumstances, the financial institution must manually post the offsetting entry to the Originatorâs account. See the AFT Settlement Part for more information.
Processing AFT Transactions at the Receiving Financial Institution
The data centre of the receiving financial institution performs its own edit check and can reject individual transactions. Rejected items appear on the Consolidated Returns, Recalls, Rejects, and Error Corrections Report â ICRR/UCRR.
The receiving financial institution posts the individual AFT transactions to the recipientsâ accounts on the due date. Although these transactions are valid, they can still be returned for reasons including (but not limited to) the following:
The recipientâs account is closed
There are insufficient funds
Returned transactions appear on the Consolidated Returns, Recalls, Rejects, and Error Corrections Report â ICRR/UCRR.
Processing Returned, Rejected, or Recalled AFT Transactions
The Originator and/or the financial institution is responsible for handling any returned, rejected, or recalled AFT transactions.
1.3	Summary of AFT Re-presentment
AFT Re-presentment is an optional service for all
PaymentStream
AFT Originators. The service allows an AFT Originator to have a returned Canadian or USD AFT debit resubmitted automatically. Although the Re-presentment service is available to
PaymentStream
AFT Originators, it runs off Central 1âs back-end AFT processing system and is not a direct feature of
PaymentStream
AFT.
A returned AFT debit can only be re-presented once and only if it was returned for the following AFT return reason codes:
901 NSF (Non-Sufficient Funds)
908 Funds Not Cleared
Requesting AFT Re-presentment Service
To request AFT re-presentment service, complete the
PaymentStream
AFT Service Application, Form 5329. See Section 5.3, Setting up Originators for AFT Re-presentment, and Section 5.4, Indicating AFT Re-presentment Preferences, for more information. Central 1 uses ServiceNow to log when an Originator ID is set up and to indicate the financial institutionâs requested start date for the re-presentment service.
AFT Re-presentment Workflow
Upon receipt of a returned AFT debit for either of the two specific return reason codes (901 or 908), Central 1âs AFT re-presentment service automatically creates a one-time AFT debit transaction for distribution back to the destination financial institution. The timing of when AFT debit returns are re-presented by Central 1 depends on the number of business days a financial institution requests for the auto re-presentment. The auto-generation of the AFT debit eliminates the need for the AFT Originator to create or resubmit a one-time only AFT debit.
Additionally, Central 1 creates the offsetting AFT credit transaction for every re-presented AFT debit that is generated. Generally, the offsetting credit is sent to the original AFT debitâs Account Number for Returns, which eliminates the need for the financial institution to manually credit the business or individual memberâs account. However, to assist with variances on how financial institutions may reconcile their AFT business membersâ accounts, financial institutions can request that the offsetting AFT credit transactions be sent to an alternate account, such as a holding account. This option can be stated when completing the request for AFT re-presentment service. See Section 5.3, Setting up Originators for AFT Re-presentment, and Section 5.4, Indicating AFT Re-presentment Preferences, for more information.
AFT Reports for AFT Re-presentment Workflow
Since AFT re-presentment is not a direct feature of
PaymentStream
AFT, there are no new AFT reports created by
PaymentStream
AFT. Existing AFT processing reports are created from Central 1âs back-end AFT processing system under the separate AFT Re-presentment Originator ID. For
PaymentStream
AFT business originators, the Central 1 AFT processing reports are included in their MERG report files under Settlement Reports.
AFT debits returned for return reason codes 901 or 908, are on the following reports:
Consolidated Returns, Recalls, Rejects and Error Corrections (ICRR/UCRR)
â Available to financial institutions and/or business members.
Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD/UCD)
â Available to financial institutions only, as Debit (J) with indicator REPRESENT.
AFT re-presented debit transaction information is on the following reports:
Summary of Data Delivered from Auto Re-presentment (ICDN/UCDN)
â Available to financial institutions and/or business members.
Summary of Data Received (ICRM/UCRM)
â Available to financial institutions and/or business members.
Item Trace Report â Incoming Items (ICTI/UCTI)
â Available to financial institutions and/or business members.
Settlement Register (ICSR/UCSR)
â Available to financial institutions only.
The offsetting credit transaction information for the AFT re-presented debit is on the following report:
Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD/UCD)
â Available to financial institutions only, as Credit (C) with the indicator REPRESENT.
If a re-presented AFT debit is returned, information is on the following reports:
Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD/UCD) report, as a returned Debit (J)
â Available to financial institutions only.
Consolidated Returns, Recalls, Rejects and Error Corrections (ICRR/UCRR) report
â Available to financial institutions and/or business members.
This ends the re-presentment cycle, since AFT debits can only be re-presented once, based on CPA Rule F1, Rules Applicable to Automated Funds Transfer (AFT) Transactions. See the AFT Reports Part for more information on settlement reports.
1.4	CPA AFT Transaction Codes
Payments Canada defines and regulates numeric AFT transaction codes that are used to describe pre-authorized debits (PADs) and direct deposits. Ensure that the correct CPA transaction code is used when creating AFT transactions. The transaction code identifies the PAD type and can affect eligibility and time frames for reimbursement claims. The current list of transaction codes is available on the Payments Canada website (
www.payments.ca
). See Standard 007, Standards for the Use of Transaction Codes and Return Reason Codes in AFT Files.
1.5	Service Charges
Each outgoing AFT transaction is subject to charges. These fees are posted to the sponsoring financial institutionâs Central 1 account and manually posted to the Originatorsâ accounts. Similarly, the financial institutionâs Central 1 account is charged for the AFT service fees (e.g., the per file charge for Originators that transmit files or manually release them on
PaymentStream
AFT or the monthly charge for Originators that automatically release files on
PaymentStream
AFT). The financial institution manually posts these charges to the Originatorsâ accounts.
The charge for incoming AFT transactions is posted monthly to the financial institutionâs Central 1 account.
There is also a one-time set-up fee for all new Originators. It is posted to the financial institutionâs Central 1 account and the financial institution determines whether to pass the fee on to its member.
Contact your central for details. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725.
Related Information
Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule,
Form 2725
(available to Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario)
